action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 14's training run produced a final validation accuracy of only ~64%, a massive drop from the prior best of 83.11%. The training logs show stable but poor convergence, indicating a bug in the rewritten train.py (likely in buffer construction or optimization setup). Analysis: Since the IN21k ViT + cosine head recipe previously worked well (iter 8-10), I must discard the buggy train.py and rewrite a clean, minimal version that faithfully implements: (1) per-task feature extraction, (2) full-feature replay buffer, (3) per-task head reinitialization, (4) AdamW + cosine annealing, (5) label smoothing & light feature noise. This should recover the ~82-83% baseline quickly (~5-6 min for 20 epochs). Decision: Rewrite train.py from scratch with verified logic, run 20 epochs, perform a local predict.py sanity check, and immediately submit for evaluation.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 20 --lr 0.001 --weight_decay 0.01 --label_smoothing 0.1 --feat_noise 0.01 && mkdir -p dummy_test && python -c "import os, torch; d=os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR','/data'); v=torch.load(os.path.join(d,'val.pt')); torch.save({'images': v['images'][:128]}, 'dummy_test/test.pt')" && python predict.py --data_path dummy_test --checkpoint checkpoints/model.pt --output dummy_out.json && python -c "import json; p=json.load(open('dummy_out.json'))['predictions']; print('num preds:', len(p)); assert len(p)==128, len(p)"
